summary: End-to-end backend + frontend testing for Harshit MadeIt portfolio. Backend: 11/11 pytest tests passed (root, contact POST happy/sad paths, contact GET sorting & no _id leak). Frontend: All critical flows verified — hero renders headline & CTAs, Book a Discovery Call correctly scrolls to #contact (Calendly URL empty), View Work scrolls to #work, 3 featured project cards with 'VIDEO COMING SOON' badges and durations (0:15, 0:10, 0:50) render, case study modal opens with all 7 sections (Overview, Objective, Storyboard, Design Frames, Motion Process, Final Video, Key Learnings), ESC + close button + backdrop click + case-study Book Call all close modal correctly, Services x5 and Process x5 render, nav links scroll, social links + email present, contact form valid submission persists to MongoDB via POST /api/contact and clears form + shows success toast.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: `logger` is referenced inside create_contact (line 95) but defined later at module level (line 127). Works because logging.basicConfig is configured at import time and root logger is available, but for clarity move the `logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)` block above the route definitions.

Empty-field contact submit uses native HTML5 `required` validation (browser-blocked) rather than the toast inside submit handler. Functionally OK (no submission, native UX), but the in-code `toast.error('Please fill in name, email and message.')` will rarely fire because the form is blocked before reaching it. Consider noValidate on the form if the custom toast is preferred, or remove the dead toast.

Backend POST /api/contact wraps the insert in try/except and returns 500 on any DB error — good, but Pydantic validation errors already short-circuit before the try block (FastAPI returns 422 automatically), so the try/except only catches DB errors. This is fine but worth noting.
Frontend FeaturedProjects/CaseStudyModal use framer-motion correctly with data-testid hooks — good testability.
seed_data_creation: Test contacts created via API: TEST_Jane Cooper, TEST_John Doe, TEST_Order1/2, TEST_UI Valid (left in DB; can be cleaned by filtering name LIKE 'TEST_%').
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test file at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py covers root + contact CRUD with create→GET persistence verification, sorting, and ObjectId-leak guard. To run: `cd /app && python -m pytest backend/tests/backend_test.py -v`. Calendly URL is intentionally empty in /app/frontend/src/pages/Portfolio.jsx — Book a Call must scroll to #contact (verified). Backdrop-click-closes-modal works at normal viewport (1920x1080); when testing at very tall viewports (e.g., 1920x4000) the modal panel may fill the entire visible area leaving no backdrop room — use normal viewport to verify. Modal section text uses `document.textContent` (not innerText) for reliable assertions since browsers cull off-screen innerText.
